architecture_fit.note: No transformer/attention mechanism at all; hand-writes a spike from batch.needle_pos, which the goal explicitly says real attempts must ignore.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies only on the benchmark's built-in uniform baseline; no explicit strawman-vs-method demonstration constructed by the attempt.

faithfulness.note: Not faithful — it reads the ground-truth needle position rather than computing attention from tokens; no causal/ablation evidence and none proposed.

operating_range.note: Sweeps 64/128/256 at perfect accuracy, but this is trivially an oracle reading the answer, so the range tells us nothing about a mechanism.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A — no hand-set Q/K/V circuit reproducing behavior; just an oracle that copies the label.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails boot-check: SyntaxError on line 64 ('return list enumerate(attn)') and missing numpy import; demo also plots a fake placeholder spike.

visualisation_rationale.note: README has a 'Why this visualisation' section, but it justifies a broken/placeholder plot that admittedly does not show real attention.

notes: Perfect automated metrics are achieved by cheating: the model_fn reads batch.needle_pos and writes a spike there, which the goal explicitly forbids, so it implements no attention mechanism and offers zero causal evidence. The app.py visualisation does not even parse (SyntaxError, missing numpy import) and its demo draws a placeholder spike unrelated to the recorded payload. Fails on architecture fit and faithfulness, the dominant rubric items.
